title: santalum haleakalae var. lanaiense
intro: Santalum haleakalae var. lanaiense, also known as the Hawaiian sandalwood, is a rare and endangered variant of the Santalum haleakalae tree, native to the islands of Hawaii. The plant contains a high concentration of santalol, a compound with potent medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Hawaiian sandalwood include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Hawaiian sandalwood is prepared as an essential oil, infused in water, or used as a tincture, and is also used in herbal teas and topical creams.
